description: UI polish pass: shrink chunky header chrome, add prominent board-level event feedback, declutter sidebar, redesign power activation toggle, and add confirm step to archetype selection.

userStories.title: Shrink game room header chrome
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game room header (title, Refresh, Back to Games) to be smaller and less dominant — so the board and hand get more visual priority.
acceptanceCriteria: In `GameRoom.tsx`, reduce the title `h1` from `text-2xl font-bold` to `text-base font-semibold` (or similar compact size)
Reduce the 'Back to Games' link to `text-xs` styling — it should be a subtle nav link, not a prominent button
Reduce the 'Refresh' button to `text-xs` styling to match
Reduce the realtime status badge to be more compact (smaller text, less padding)
The header row and status row combined should take up noticeably less vertical space than before
All existing functionality (navigation, refresh, realtime indicator) still works
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Board-level event feedback callouts
userStories.description: As a player, I want brief animated callouts to appear near the board when events happen (Mists roll, captures, Plus, Elemental) — so I immediately understand what happened without having to check the sidebar. The callouts should feel like part of the game: use the same Curse of Strahd flavor and animation style as VictoryOverlay/DefeatOverlay/BoardGrid captures.
acceptanceCriteria: When a card is placed and Mists triggers Fog or Omen, show a floating callout overlaying the board area that auto-fades after ~2 seconds. Fog: use a cold/ominous style (blue-gray tones, e.g. 'The Mists cloud your vision… −2' or similar thematic text). Omen: use a radiant/empowered style (purple/gold tones, e.g. 'The Mists favor you! +2')

When captures occur, show a callout near the board. Single capture: something understated. Combo (2+): make it dramatic — e.g. 'Chain! ×N claimed' with a stepped scale-in animation matching the VictoryOverlay's steppedEase pattern
When Plus triggers, show a prominent 'Plus!' callout on the board area (cyan), with a brightness flash or glow similar to the capture flip's brightness burst in BoardGrid
When Elemental triggers, show the element name callout (gold/yellow) with the element's symbol from ELEMENT_SYMBOLS (e.g. '🩸 Blood Elemental!')

Use `font-heading` for callout text, `drop-shadow` or `text-shadow` for depth (match VictoryOverlay/DefeatOverlay style). Use `steppedEase` for at least the entry animation to maintain the pixel-art-inspired feel
Callouts use motion/react AnimatePresence with enter/exit animations. Entry should feel punchy (scale overshoot like the combo counter's initial scale 1.5 → 1), exit should fade smoothly
Position callouts as an absolutely-positioned overlay on the board container with `pointer-events-none` so they never block cell clicks
The sidebar retains the persistent last-move info (Mists roll, capture count, etc.) — the board callouts are supplementary, not a replacement
Add a frontend test: verify board-level callout renders when last_move has mists_effect='fog'
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Declutter sidebar: archetype powers as tooltips
userStories.description: As a player, I want the sidebar to be less information-dense — archetype power descriptions should be available on hover/tap rather than always visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the `ArchetypePowerAside` blocks from the sidebar's always-visible content
In the archetype status section (Your archetype / Opponent archetype), make the archetype name a hover target that shows the power description in a tooltip
Tooltip should show the power title and power text from `archetypesCopy.ts`
Tooltip works on both desktop (hover) and mobile (tap/long-press or a small info icon tap)
Compact the opponent hand count + archetype status into a tighter layout (consider a single row or two-column compact grid)
The sidebar should feel noticeably less cluttered after this change
Existing archetype info (name, used/available status) remains visible without hovering
Add a frontend test: verify tooltip content appears on hover for an archetype
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Prominent power activation toggle
userStories.description: As a player, I want the archetype power activation to be a large, visually distinct button instead of a subtle checkbox — so I never accidentally miss that my power is available.
acceptanceCriteria: In `ActionPanel.tsx`, replace the checkbox `Use Power` with a prominent toggle button (e.g. '⚡ Use Power')
When power is available and not yet toggled, the button should have a subtle pulse or glow animation to draw attention
When toggled ON, the button should be visually distinct (filled/highlighted state) so the player clearly sees power is active
When power has been used (`archetype_used: true`), the button should not appear (same as current behavior)
Direction pickers (Skulker N/E/S/W, Martial CW/CCW) still appear below the button when toggled on
The button should be large enough to be clearly visible but not so large it crowds the hand panel in the side-by-side layout
Add a frontend test: verify the power toggle button renders with pulse animation class when power is available
`bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Archetype selection confirm button
userStories.description: As a player, I want a confirm step when selecting my archetype — so I don't accidentally pick one by misclicking.
acceptanceCriteria: In `ArchetypeModal.tsx`, clicking an archetype button should highlight/select it without immediately submitting
Add a 'Confirm' button at the bottom of the modal that submits the selected archetype
The confirm button should be disabled until an archetype is selected
The archetype preview panel (right side) still updates on hover/focus as before
The selected archetype button should have a visually distinct selected state (e.g. ring/outline) separate from hover
Pressing Confirm calls `onSelectArchetype` (same as the current direct-click behavior)
The pending/disabled state during submission still works (Confirm button shows loading state)
Add a frontend test: verify clicking an archetype does NOT immediately call onSelectArchetype, and clicking Confirm does
`bun run test --run` passes
